IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: To promote and assist the educational and public service functions of CSU, Chico.
IRS990.MissionDesc: To promote and assist the educational and public service functions of CSU, Chico by administering grants, farm operations and other activities.
IRS990.Desc: THE GENERATION AND ADMINISTRATION OF OVER 400 EXTERNALLY FUNDED TEACHING, RESEARCH AND SERVICE PROJECTS FROM FEDERAL, STATE, AND PRIVATE SOURCES (CONTRACTS AND GRANTS) EACH YEAR. THE MAJORITY OF THESE "SPONSORED PROGRAMS" PROVIDE EDUCATIONAL OPPORTUNITIES FOR STUDENTS AS WELL.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: CAMPUS PROGRAMS OPERATE TO SUPPORT THE MISSION OF CHICO STATE ENTERPRISES AND SUPPORT CSU, CHICO. THESE PROGRAMS INCLUDE NON-CREDIT BEARING REGIONAL AND CONTINUING EDUCATION ACTIVITIES, AND UNIVERSITY PERFORMANCES WHICH PROVIDE BENEFITS TO BOTH CAMPUS AND THE COMMUNITY.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: ADMINISTRATIVE SUPPORT IS PROVIDED TO ALL PROGRAMS OPERATING THROUGH CHICO STATE ENTERPRISES. THESE PROGRAMS PROVIDE EDUCATIONAL OPPORTUNITIES AND INCOME IN SUPPORT OF THE ACADEMIC PROGRAM. ADMINISTRATIVE SUPPORT IS ALSO GIVEN TO THE UNIVERSITY FOUNDATION, WHICH HANDLES ALL PHILANTHROPIC FUNDS FOR CSU, CHICO.

Form990PartVIISectionAGrp.TitleTxt: Board Treasurer/CSU Chico VP Business and Finance

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule A, Part IV, Section A, Line 2 Supported Org. Without IRS Status 509(a)1 or (2)
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: The supported organization of Chico State Enterprises is a State University, and therefore does not have an IRS determination of status under Section 509(a)(1) or (2).

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule D, Part X, Line 2 FIN 48 (ASC 740) footnote
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Chico State Enterprises is exempt from income taxes under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code and Section 23701(d) of the California Revenue and Taxation Code. However, income from certain activities not directly related to the tax-exempt purpose is subject to taxation as unrelated business income. Expenses have exceeded the business income; accordingly, no provision for income taxes has been recorded. CSE functionally supports CSU, Chico and has been classified in accordance with Section 509(a)(3), Type I. CSE accounts for income taxes in accordance with FASB ASC 740, Income Taxes, which clarifies the accounting for uncertainty in income taxes recognized in CSE's financial statements and prescribes a recognition threshold and measurement attribute for the financial statement recognition and measurement of a tax position taken or expected to be taken in a tax return. FASB ASC 740, Income Taxes, also provides guidance on derecognition and measurement of a tax position taken or expected to be taken in a tax return. Based on this analysis, a liability is recorded if uncertain tax benefits have been received. CSE's practice is to recognize interest and penalties, if any, related to uncertain tax positions in the tax expense.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule D, Part XI, Line 2(d) Other revenues in audited financial statements not in form 990
Schedule D, Part XI, Line 4(b) Other revenues in form 990 not in audited financial statements
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Reclass contribution to affiliate - 1300 FS reclass for debt service - -1053

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule D, Part XII, Line 2(d) Other expenses in audited financial statements not in form 990
Schedule D, Part XII, Line 4(b) Other expenses in form 990 not in audited financial statements
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Reclass contribution to affiliate - 1300 FS reclass for debt service - -1053

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule F, Part I, Line 3 Method used to account for expenditures on org's financial statements
Schedule I, Part I, Line 2 Procedures for monitoring use of grant funds.
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: CHICO STATE ENTERPRISES HAS ESTABLISHED POLICIES AND PROCEDURES TO ENSURE GRANT FUNDS ARE DISTRIBUTED PROPERLY. ADDITIONALLY, CHICO STATE ENTERPRISES UNDERGOES SEVERAL AUDITS BY FUNDING AGENCIES THROUGHOUT THE YEAR. FURTHERMORE, CHICO STATE ENTERPRISES IS AUDITED ANNUALLY BY AN INDEPENDENT FIRM AND TRI-ANNUALLY BY THE CHANCELLOR'S OFFICE TO ENSURE COMPLIANCE WITH THESE POLICIES AND PROCEDURES.

RltdOrgOfficerTrstKeyEmplGrp.TitleTxt: Board Treasurer/CSU Chico VP Business and Finance

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule M, Part I Explanations of reporting method for number of contributions
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Other - Lambs NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS Other - Trees NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTIONS

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 11b Review of form 990 by governing body
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: An electronic copy of the Form 990 is sent to the governing board prior to filing with the IRS. If changes are necessary, the revised 990 is redistributed prior to filing; otherwise, the Form 990 is filed.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 12c Conflict of interest policy
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The governing board members and employees are required to receive and sign an annual Conflict of Interest Statement which is maintained by the CSE administrative office. To avoid contracts or transactions entered into by the governing borad of CSE, a Board of Directors member must: 1) disclose any financial interest which could be affected by the action of the Board of Directors and 2) Refrain from any action to influence or approve such a transaction. The CSE Chief Executive Officer shall provide the CSU, Chico Chief Financial Officer with a report of compliance with this policy. This report should include the names , positions, terms of office and the date the compliance statement were signed. This report shall be forwarded to the CSU, Chico Chief Financial Officer annually by June 30th. Violations of the Conflict of Interest Policy shall be reported to the CSU, Chico Chief Financial Officer.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 15a Process to establish compensation of top management official
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Board of Directors approves compensation based on the CSU system-wide comparability data as well as regional and local bench marking comparability data. If any Board Member has a conflict of interest regarding compensation, the Board Member may not participate in, or influence, the decision making process. The last such compensation decision was in 2022.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 15b Process to establish compensation of other employees
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Board of Directors approves compensation based on the CSU system-wide comparability data as well as regional and local bench marking comparability data. If any Board Member has a conflict of interest regarding compensation, the Board Member may not participate in, or influence, the decision making process. The last such compensation decision was in 2022.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Line 19 Required documents available to the public
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The CSE's governing documents, conflict of interest policy, tax returns and financial statements are available to the public upon request and via the organization's website.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part IX, Line 11g Other Fees
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Contract services - Total Expense: 13905391, Program Service Expense: 12979066, Management and General Expenses: 926325, Fundraising Expenses: ;
